text: After visualizing the tumor through VATS, we begin by cutting the costal pleura over the inferior most attachment of the tumor mass.
Next, we dissect and cut the mediastinal pleura over the descending thoracic aorta and this structure is in close proximity to the pericardium.
We then lift the tumor over the posterior lateral part of the chest wall, first over the vertebra, second over the inner surface of the ribs.
Meticulous dissection is needed to prevent any post-operative bleeding complications.
We then lift the tumor over the thoracic aorta and dissect in the proper plane
Any vessels encountered are coagulated with the help of the liquor shower.
This is the aorta and the vertebral column.
Lastly, any remaining attachments are cut ensuring that no remnant of the tumour is left behind.
Hemostasis is secured, a thorough wash given, ICD placed and the tumour is removed through a 3 cm small incision.
